mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2015 payroll level at 130,910k suggests ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: post-recession expansion with stable employment growth
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains positive but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25% after an extended period near zero, it signals the Fed's confidence in economic recovery and the end of emergency monetary accommodation. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice equity risk premiums upward in response to tightening expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% after 36+ months below 0.20%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of June 2015, near historical lows
regime_hint: transition from zero-bound monetary policy to normalization cycle
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% threshold, or the magnitude of negative returns is less than 0.5% on average.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating an economy losing momentum. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as both demand pillars deteriorate simultaneously, reducing corporate earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines >2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls >5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352,985M and HOUST at 574k units as of May-June 2015
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with synchronized demand weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST declines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals accelerating manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as supply-side strength translates into earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3043 (2017=100) as of June 2015, near post-crisis peak
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial momentum
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 0.5% month-over-month does not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 0.50%, it creates a negative real interest rate environment that erodes bond returns and forces investors into equities as a real asset hedge. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in low-rate, moderate-inflation regimes.
regime_hint: low-rate environment with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets immediately reprice earnings expectations downward in response to labor market weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% after 6+ months below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% as of June 2015 snapshot, though actual July 2015 rate was ~5.3%
regime_hint: transition from tight labor market to loosening conditions
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5% threshold, or the magnitude of negative returns is less than 0.3% on average.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals an economy losing momentum toward potential growth, which reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0% after 4+ quarters above 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,973.0B (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2015
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% threshold, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000k annualized and retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals synchronized strength in consumer and construction demand, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as both demand pillars expand simultaneously, supporting corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST annualized rate > 1,000k AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 574k units and RSAFS at 352,985M as of May-June 2015
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS acceleration.
